title: commersonia platycalyx
intro: Commersonia platycalyx, also known as the native quandong, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. It is traditionally used in bush medicine, where the leaves, bark, and fruit have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, sore eyes, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of the plant are attributed to its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Commersonia platycalyx is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
